---
title: "498 Project"  
author: "Lei Wei"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  pdf_document:
    latex_engine: xelatex  
header-includes:
  - \usepackage{ctex}  
  - \usepackage{graphicx}  
  - \usepackage{booktabs}  
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
# 1. Load Required Libraries
library(tidyverse)
library(ggdist)
library(ggcorrplot)
library(viridis)
library(scales)
library(knitr) # For clean table output

# 2. Load Processed Data
df_final <- read_csv("Air_Quality_and_Health_Impacts_processed.csv")

# 3. Label Shortening Helper (Ensures clean correlation plots)
shorten_labels <- function(x) {
  x %>%
    str_replace_all("Fine particles \\(PM 2.5\\)", "PM2.5") %>%
    str_replace_all("Nitrogen dioxide \\(NO2\\)", "NO2") %>%
    str_replace_all("Ozone \\(O3\\)", "Ozone") %>%
    str_replace_all("Asthma emergency department visits due to", "Asthma_ED") %>%
    str_replace_all("Annual vehicle miles traveled", "VMT") %>%
    str_replace_all("Cardiovascular hospitalizations due to", "Cardio_Hosp") %>%
    str_replace_all("Deaths due to", "Mortality") %>%
    str_trunc(25) %>%
    make.unique()
}

# =================================================================
# SECTION 1: VISUALIZATIONS (Saved as Independent High-Res Files)
# =================================================================

# PLOT 1: Top 10 Monitoring Indicators (Clean Blue Bar Chart)
p1 <- df_final %>%
  count(Name) %>%
  top_n(10, n) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(Name, n), y = n)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "#2c7fb8", width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = n), hjust = -0.2, size = 3.5, fontface = "bold") +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = function(x) str_wrap(x, width = 45)) +
  labs(title = "Top 10 Environmental Indicators by Record Count",
       subtitle = "Data source: UHF42 Special Study (2009-2023)",
       x = "Indicator Name", y = "Total Records") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

ggsave("Plot1_Top_Indicators.png", p1, width = 12, height = 7)

# PLOT 2: PM2.5 Burden - Socio-economic Proxy (Boxplot)
p2 <- df_final %>%
  filter(Name == "Fine particles (PM 2.5)") %>%
  filter(`Geo Type Name` == "UHF42") %>%
  mutate(Income_Group = ifelse(str_detect(`Geo Place Name`, "Hunts Point|Mott Haven|Harlem|Bedford Stuyvesant"), 
                               "Low Income Area", "High Income Area")) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Income_Group, y = `Data Value`, fill = Income_Group)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.8, width = 0.4, outlier.alpha = 0.3) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("High Income Area" = "#2c7fb8", "Low Income Area" = "#7fcdbb")) +
  labs(title = "PM2.5 Concentration: Socio-economic Comparison",
       subtitle = "Comparing identified Low-Income proxies vs High-Income areas",
       x = "Neighborhood Socio-economic Proxy", y = "PM2.5 Concentration (mcg/m3)") +
  theme_classic(base_size = 14)

ggsave("Plot2_PM25_Income_Equity.png", p2, width = 9, height = 6)

# PLOT 3: Indicator Correlation Bubble Matrix
df_wide <- df_final %>%
  filter(`Geo Type Name` == "UHF42") %>%
  group_by(`Geo Place Name`, Name) %>%
  summarise(Avg_Value = mean(`Data Value`, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = 'drop') %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Name, values_from = Avg_Value)

colnames(df_wide) <- shorten_labels(colnames(df_wide))
corr_matrix <- cor(df_wide %>% select(where(is.numeric)), use = "pairwise.complete.obs")

p3 <- ggcorrplot(corr_matrix, 
                 hc.order = TRUE, type = "lower", lab = TRUE, lab_size = 3,
                 method = "circle", 
                 colors = c("#2c7fb8", "white", "#e6550d"),
                 title = "Correlation Matrix: Air Quality vs. Health Outcomes") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

ggsave("Plot3_Correlation_Matrix.png", p3, width = 12, height = 11)

# PLOT 4: Spatial PM2.5 Distribution (Advanced Raincloud Plot)
p4 <- df_final %>%
  filter(Name == "Fine particles (PM 2.5)") %>%
  filter(!is.na(`Geo Type Name`)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = `Geo Type Name`, y = `Data Value`, fill = `Geo Type Name`)) +
  stat_halfeye(adjust = .5, width = .6, justification = -.3, .width = 0, point_colour = NA) +
  geom_boxplot(width = .1, outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_point(position = position_jitter(width = .1), alpha = 0.2, size = 0.6) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "mako", begin = 0.3) +
  labs(title = "PM2.5 Density Distribution Across Geo Scales",
       subtitle = "Comparing variation between Borough, CD, and UHF42 levels",
       x = "Geographic Category", y = "Concentration (Processed)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

ggsave("Plot4_PM25_Raincloud.png", p4, width = 11, height = 7)


# =================================================================
# SECTION 2: TABULAR SUMMARIES (Console Output)
# =================================================================

cat("\n--- TABLE 1: Descriptive Statistics for Key Pollutants ---\n")
summary_stats <- df_final %>%
  filter(Name %in% c("Fine particles (PM 2.5)", "Nitrogen dioxide (NO2)", "Ozone (O3)")) %>%
  group_by(Name) %>%
  summarise(
    Records = n(),
    Mean = round(mean(`Data Value`, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    SD = round(sd(`Data Value`, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    Min = round(min(`Data Value`, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    Max = round(max(`Data Value`, na.rm = TRUE), 2)
  )
print(summary_stats)

cat("\n--- TABLE 2: Top 10 Polluted Neighborhoods (PM2.5 Avg) ---\n")
hotspot_neighborhoods <- df_final %>%
  filter(Name == "Fine particles (PM 2.5)", `Geo Type Name` == "UHF42") %>%
  group_by(`Geo Place Name`) %>%
  summarise(Avg_PM25 = round(mean(`Data Value`, na.rm = TRUE), 3)) %>%
  arrange(desc(Avg_PM25)) %>%
  head(10)
print(hotspot_neighborhoods)

cat("\n--- TABLE 3: Outlier Frequency by Borough ---\n")
outlier_table <- df_final %>%
  filter(Is_Outlier == 1) %>%
  group_by(`Geo Type Name`) %>%
  count(name = "Outlier_Count") %>%
  arrange(desc(Outlier_Count))
print(outlier_table)

cat("\nProcessing Complete. 4 High-Res PNG files and 3 Summary Tables generated.\n")
```
